title: IETF Internet-Draft: Bolyra Mutual Authentication Protocol

artifacts.description: Complete IETF Internet-Draft in RFC 7749 (xml2rfc) plain-text output format covering all normative sections
Source xml2rfc v3 markup file for the Internet-Draft, suitable for submission to datatracker.ietf.org
Canonical glossary: identity commitment, credential commitment, scope commitment, nullifier, delegation chain, phantom-delegatee, stale-root — cross-referenced to draft sections
CDDL (RFC 8610) schema for all CBOR-encoded proof payloads, public signals, and protocol messages
ASCII message sequence chart (MSC) for the enrollment flow: Holder ↔ Issuer ↔ Accumulator Service
ASCII MSC for the mutual authentication handshake flow: Prover ↔ Verifier, including nonce exchange and nullifier submission
Normative circuit specification references: identity circuit, credential circuit, scope circuit — input/output signals, constraint counts, and conformance requirements
Standalone security analysis covering phantom-delegatee attack, stale-root vulnerability, nullifier linkability, and replay mitigations — feeds into draft §Security Considerations
CBOR-encoded test vectors for enrollment proof, handshake proof, and negative cases (expired root, replayed nullifier)
IANA registration requests: CBOR tag for proof payload, media type application/bolyra-proof+cbor, and algorithm registry entries

steps: Collect and canonicalize all existing protocol artifacts (circuits, contracts, SDK types) to extract authoritative terminology and signal names
Draft §1 Introduction and §2 Conventions: establish RFC 2119/8174 normative language boilerplate, document scope, and intended use cases (credit-union mutual auth, portable identity)
Draft §3 Terminology: define identity commitment, credential commitment, scope commitment, nullifier, delegation chain, phantom-delegatee, stale-root with precise mathematical notation
Draft §4 Protocol Overview: high-level two-phase description (enrollment, handshake) with informative narrative and references to MSC diagrams
Produce enrollment MSC (msc-enrollment.txt): Holder→Issuer credential issuance, accumulator insertion, commitment broadcast, confirmation receipt
Produce handshake MSC (msc-handshake.txt): Verifier nonce challenge, Prover proof generation, proof submission, nullifier check, accept/reject response
Draft §5 Wire Format: normative CBOR (RFC 8949) encoding for ProofPayload, PublicSignals, EnrollmentRequest, HandshakeRequest, HandshakeResponse — with CDDL schema in wire-format.cddl
Draft §6 Circuit Specification References: normative constraints on identity, credential, and scope circuits — required public inputs, output commitments, and groth16/plonk agility
Draft §7 Security Considerations: phantom-delegatee (delegatee key binding gap), stale-root (accumulator epoch freshness), nullifier replay, proof malleability, side-channel leakage
Draft §8 IANA Considerations: CBOR tag registration, media type registration, algorithm identifiers
Draft §9 References: normative (RFC 2119, RFC 8949, RFC 8610, RFC 7517, Groth16 paper) and informative
Assemble xml2rfc v3 source (draft-bolyra-mutual-auth-00.xml) from all section drafts and validate with xml2rfc toolchain
Generate plain-text output (draft-bolyra-mutual-auth-00.txt) and verify idnits compliance
Produce CBOR conformance test vectors (conformance-vectors.json) covering happy-path enrollment, happy-path handshake, stale-root rejection, and nullifier replay rejection
Cross-check all signal names in circuit-spec.md against actual circuit source files and reconcile any discrepancies
Internal review pass: verify all MUST/SHALL/SHOULD/MAY usage is consistent and each normative claim is backed by a circuit or wire-format constraint
Update terminology.md with final section cross-references and publish as standalone companion document
dependencies: Finalized Circom circuit source files (identity, credential, scope) with confirmed public input/output signal names
Canonical accumulator contract interface (root update cadence, epoch definition)
Nullifier registry interface specification (submission, deduplication semantics)
Agreement on proof system agility scope (Groth16-only v1 vs. PLONK extension point)
CBOR tag range allocation decision (private range vs. IANA early allocation)
